6月11日，哥斯达黎加斯醒来了一个神秘的新应用程序图标，阅读“Ministerio de Salud de Costa Rica”，在他们的Android手机上没有许可。右翼和其他政府持怀疑态度之间的反应特别指出。 “去他妈的自己，你不能闯入私人财产，”写一个推特用户。
 几小时内，谜团被揭示：卫生部宣布它是谷歌和苹果公司开发的联系追踪计划的一部分。哥斯达黎加是世界上推出该倡议的第一个国家，并且在政府能够宣布之前，系统更新被错误地描绘了作为应用程序的图标。已经在工程处于花费的卷展栏被几个错误的像素沸腾。
 “Malditas Ratas在未经我们的同意和背后安装申请，”另一名推特用户写了一位名叫迭戈Vargas的计算机技术人员，他宣称自己是他生物中的“反女性，反逐步”。 vargas告诉世界其他地区，当他看到人们抱怨这个应用程序的推特时，他去检查他的Android手机。它没有出现在主页上。然而，作为IT工作者，他知道在他所有申请的系统列表下，它是 - 哥斯达黎加卫生部，12.28MB。他的父母也有Android用户。
 “我在手机上安装了应用程序，包括更新甚至谷歌应用程序，”他说。 “但是这个应用程序在我的手机上就是这样。”
 2020年4月，随着世界各地的大流行，苹果和谷歌加入势力，开发一个可跨设备可操作的联系方式。该计划是与全球公共卫生官员合作，利用蓝牙技术，帮助政府通知公众对病毒的暴露。
 哥斯达黎加政府渴望提前参与。哥斯达黎加科学，技术和电信部的数字治理总监Jorge Mora非常适合该计划的潜力，但理解人们会谨慎下载他们的手机上的跟踪应用程序。 “这些类型的全球范围的应用程序具有一定的效用，而且面临抗抵抗力，”他告诉世界其他地区。
 但两家科技公司也在开发另一种合同追踪方法：曝光通知表达，一个不要求人们下载单独应用的系统。相反，它将直接编程到手机的设备设置中。然后，用户将能够从“设置”页面上或“关闭”或“默认设置”在“OFF”模式下打开或关闭。 
它有点像iPhone上臭名昭着的预下载的U2专辑背后的想法：文件会在那里，但是它可以通过用户激活它。
 6月，哥斯达黎加被设定为推出新系统的世界第一所在地。莫拉很兴奋，通过将接触追踪内置在手机的操作设置中，摩擦显着较低。用户会收到警报并选择是否打开或关闭它。 “它让我们有机会真正产生透明信息，”他说。一切都根据苹果设备的计划。 6月17日，在程序启动后，iPhone用户收到了曝光通知的通知。
   这并没有发生在许多Android手机上。 6月11日，在政府宣布主动前，Android用户看到了新图标。莫拉称之为“意外行为”从平台 - 换句话说，一个错误。
 “我们了解有关一些Android手机的报告，显示了他们的家庭屏幕上的配置错误或曝光通知应用程序，”谷歌代表告诉世界其他地位。
 “这不是我们想要的推出，”莫拉说。 “它对积极计划带来了负面情绪。”他看着人们采取社交媒体，表达他们对侵入的挫败感，以及在代表的图标上的阴谋理论。阴谋论证似乎是在马萨诸塞州发生同样拙劣的Covid-19跟踪发射时加强。
 拙劣的发射负面影响整个计划。哥斯达黎加斯担心他们已经自动注册了政府计划。像莫拉这样的专家知道追踪者不是一个应用程序，更不用说政府监测装置，但他无力阻止出现的叙事。据Mora称，现在已经完成了损坏：采用追踪软件的速度比Costa Rican政府慢。
 对于Vargas，计算机技术人员，它都证实了他的政府意识，夸大和监督。在他在手机上看到了健康图标之后，他将他的Twitter横幅改为来自蝙蝠侠特许经营权的一个角色的屏幕截图，看起来令人沮丧的屏幕屏幕。 Vargas已经阅读了政府的解释，但他不打算很快就计划在系统中选择。 “随着他们所做的一切，而不是在大流行中造成伤害，诚实地是另一个谎言，”他告诉世界其他地区。
note_en:
On June 11, Costa Ricans awoke to a mysterious new app icon, reading “Ministerio de Salud de Costa Rica,” appearing on their Android phones without their permission. The reaction among right-wing and other government-skeptical crowds was particularly pointed. “Go fuck yourself, you can’t break into private property,”  wrote one Twitter user.
 Within hours, the mystery was revealed: the Ministry of Health  announced it was part of a contact tracing program developed by Google and Apple. Costa Rica was the first country in the world to launch the initiative, and a system update mistakenly portrayed the icon as an app before the government was able to announce it. The rollout, which had been in the works for months, was botched by a few errant pixels.
 “ Malditas ratas installing applications without our consent and behind our backs,”  wrote another Twitter user, a computer technician named Diego Vargas, who declared himself to be “anti-feminazis, anti-progressive” in his bio. Vargas told  Rest of World that when he saw people complaining on Twitter about the app, he went to check his Android phone. It did not appear on the homepage. As an IT worker, though, he knew to look under the system list of all his applications, and there it was — Ministry of Health of Costa Rica, 12.28MB. His parents, also Android users, had it too.
 “I have the installation of apps disabled on my phone, including updates and even Google applications,” he said. “But this app appeared on my phone just like that.”
 In April 2020, as the pandemic spread throughout the world, Apple and Google joined forces to  develop a contact tracing platform that would be operable across devices. The plan was to work with public health officials across the world, utilizing bluetooth technology to help governments inform the public about their exposure to the virus.
 The Costa Rican government was eager to get involved early. Jorge Mora, director of digital governance at Costa Rica’s Ministry of Science, Technology, and Telecommunications, was enthusiastic about the potential of the program, but understood that people would be wary of downloading tracing apps on their phones. “These types of applications at the global level have had a certain utility, but also face resistance,” he told  Rest of World.
 But the two tech companies were also developing another method of contract tracing:  Exposure Notifications Express, a system that does not require people to download a separate application. Instead, it would be programmed directly into a phone’s device settings. Users would then be able to toggle it on or off right from the settings page, with the default setting being in the “off” mode.
 It was a bit like the idea behind the infamous  pre-downloaded U2 album on the iPhone: The file would be there, but it would be up to the user to activate it.
 In June, Costa Rica was set to become the first location in the world to launch the new system. Mora was excited that, by having the contact tracing built into phones’ operating settings, there would be significantly less friction. Users would receive an alert and choose whether to turn it on or off. “It gives us the opportunity to really generate a message of transparency,” he said.  Everything went according to plan on Apple devices. On June 17, right after the program launched, iPhone users  received a notification that Exposure Notifications were available.
   This did not happen on many Android phones. On June 11, before the government announced the initiative, Android users saw the new icon. Mora called it “unexpected behavior” from the platform — in other words, a bug.
 “We are aware of reports on some Android phones showing configuration errors or an Exposure Notifications app on their home screens,” a Google representative told  Rest of World.
 “It wasn’t the rollout we wanted for the product,” Mora said. “It came with a negative emotion for a positive program.” He watched as people took to social media, expressing their frustration with the intrusion, as well as conspiracy theories over what the icon represented. The conspiracist argument seemed to be reinforced when, the next week, a similarly botched Covid-19 tracking launch  occurred in Massachusetts.
 The botched launch negatively affected the entire program. Costa Ricans worried they had been automatically enrolled in a government scheme. Experts like Mora knew that the tracker wasn’t an app, let alone a governmental monitoring device, but he was powerless to stop the emerging narrative. The damage had now been done: Adoption of the tracing software has been slower than the Costa Rican government hoped for, according to Mora.
 For Vargas, the computer technician, it all confirmed his sense of government overreach and surveillance. After he saw the Ministry of Health icon on his phone, he changed his Twitter banner to a screenshot of a character from the Batman franchise, looking dismayed at a wall of surveillance screens.   Vargas has read the explanations from the government, but he doesn’t plan on opting in to the system any time soon. “With everything they have done to harm instead of benefit the population with the pandemic, honestly it’s just another lie,” he told  Rest of World.
title: 谷歌在哥斯达黎加的手机上自动安装了一个Covid-19跟踪器
title_en: Google automatically installed a Covid-19 tracker on phones in Costa Rica

在一项新的研究中，国家卫生研究员研究人员报告说，2020年春季和夏季的Covid-19在美国的普遍存在的案例远远超过了已知的病例，并且感染影响了国家的影响。对于每次诊断的Covid-19案例，研究人员估计有4.8个未确诊的案件，仅占7月份的额外1680万个案件。该团队对没有先前诊断的SARS-COV-2感染的人的血液样本以及社会经济，健康和人口统计数据的分析，可以深入了解病毒和亚组脆弱性对未确诊的感染的漏洞。
 “这项研究有助于解释病毒传播到国家和全球各地的速度如何迅速，”Bruce Tromberg，Ph.D.）国家生物医学成像研究所和生物工程研究所（Nibib）主任，其中一家NIH研究所谁运行NIH SARS-COV-2 Seroprevalence项目。 “这些信息将是非常宝贵的，因为我们评估了保持人们安全的最佳公共卫生措施，因为新甚至更传播的变体出现和疫苗抗体反应随着时间的推移而变化。”
 除了NIBIB之外，研究团队还包括来自国家过敏和传染病研究所（NIAID）的科学家，国家推进翻译科学中心（NCATS）;和国家癌症学院（NCI）赞助的癌症研究的弗雷德里克国家实验室。他们在2021年6月22日的报告，早期在线翻译医学问题代表了来自2020年4月推出的12个月NIH研究的第一个数据。
 “冠状病毒大流行的标志是人们感染了导致Covid-19有很少或没有症状的病毒，”传染病的实验室，临床研究单位Matthew J. Memoli说，Matthew J. Memoli说，婚礼。 “在计算美国的症状人数的同时，对大流行和公共卫生反应的影响是至关重要的，因为对Covid-19流行的全面赞赏需要计算未经认可的人。”
 Covid-19疾病可以从短暂的咳嗽，疲劳和发烧到严重的疾病，这可能导致住院和死亡。无症状SARS-COV-2感染很少被诊断，是病毒蔓延的静音源。虽然没有引起明显的疾病迹象，但未确诊的感染对公众构成了危险，并且是需要公共卫生资源和解决大流行的策略的关键因素。
 该团队从全国各地招募了超过240,000名志愿者，然后通过配额抽样从该池中选择了8,058人，以确保他们的学习队列是美国人口的代表。每位参与者都收到样品套件，以返回NIH，用DAB的干血或在NIH绘制的血液，其中大多数他们在10月10日至7月31日之间提供的11周跨度。参与者还完成了一个调查问卷，使得进一步分析研究结果。
 研究人员使用酶联免疫吸附试验来检测识别来自SARS-COV-2病毒的蛋白质区的抗体，称为抗原。血液样品中的抗体可以与这些抗原结合。该团队发现，大约8,000个血液样本中的304个是血清阳性，这意味着它们含有针对SARS-COV-2病毒的抗体。研究人员估计，美国成年人的4.6％在学习期间有未确诊的Covid-19。他们确定的每个诊断的Covid-19案例对应于在此时间框架期间估计的4.8个未确诊的疾病病例。 
最年轻的参与者 - 年龄的年龄至18岁至44岁 - 具有最高估计的血清阳性，5.9％，
    中大西洋和东北地区的参与者的率最高（分别为8.6％和7.5％），中西部的参与者的率最低（1.6％），
    黑人/非洲裔美国受访者估计最高的血清阳性率（14.2％），其次是美国原住民/阿拉斯加本地（6.8％），西班牙裔（6.1％），白色/白种人（2.5％）和亚洲（2％）受访者。
 “在7月20日中期的美国康迪德-19案件的估计，3.3亿人口300万，应在包括无症状阳性结果的百分比时向上调近2000万，”高级共计作者Kaitlyn Sadtler，Ph.D.，Immunogineing的Nibib段首席。 “当时已知病例与这些无症状感染之间的这种差距不仅具有回顾性地理解这种大流行，而且影响未来的流行性准备。”
 研究人员目前正在追随注册的参与者，以评估Seroprevalence的六个月和12个月地位;它将包括新分析，以区分从疫苗接种的感染与抗体的抗体，以及与关注的变体的抗体反应性。
 新报告是NIH SARS-COV-2 SEROPREVALING项目的一部分。该协作包括由Matthew J. Memoli，M.D.的研究组在NIAID; Matthew D. Hall，Ph.D.，NCATS的早期翻译分支; Dominic Esposito，Ph.D.，Frederick国家癌症研究实验室的蛋白表达实验室;和悲伤，关于免疫工程，铌的部分。
 该研究得到了NIH Intramural Recharle Research计划的支持，部分基于合同号HHSN261200800001E，75N91019D00024，任务订单号75N91019F00130，临床和翻译科学奖项计划拨款UL1TR003096（伯明翰大学罗伯特P.Kimberly，MD，伯明翰大学）和UL1TR001857（斯图文E.Reis，MD，匹兹堡大学）。 
关于国家生物医学成像与生物工程研究所（NIBIB）：NIBIB的使命是通过引发发展和加速生物医学技术的应用来改善健康。该研究所致力于将工程和物理科学与生物学和药物融合，以推进我们对疾病的理解及其预防，检测，诊断和治疗。 NIBIB支持内部实验室内的新兴技术研发，并通过补助金，合作和培训。 Nibib网站提供更多信息。
 关于国家卫生研究院（NIH）：NIH，Nation＆＃39; S医学研究机构，包括27个机构和中心，是美国卫生和人类服务部的一部分。 NIH是主要联邦机构进行基础，临床和翻译医学研究，并正在研究常见和罕见疾病的原因，治疗和治疗。有关NIH及其程序的更多信息，请访问www.nih.gov。
note_en:
In a new study, National Institutes of Health researchers report that the prevalence of COVID-19 in the United States during spring and summer of 2020 far exceeded the known number of cases and that infection affected the country unevenly. For every diagnosed COVID-19 case in this time frame, the researchers estimate that there were 4.8 undiagnosed cases, representing an additional 16.8 million cases by July alone. The team’s analysis of blood samples from people who did not have a previously diagnosed SARS-CoV-2 infection, along with socioeconomic, health, and demographic data, offers insight into the undetected spread of the virus and subgroup vulnerability to undiagnosed infection.
 “This study helps account for how quickly the virus spread to all corners of the country and the globe,” said Bruce Tromberg, Ph.D., director of the National Institute of Biomedical Imaging and Bioengineering (NIBIB), one of the NIH institutes who run the NIH SARS-CoV-2 Seroprevalence Project. “The information will be invaluable as we assess the best public health measures needed to keep people safe, as new—and even more transmissible—variants emerge and vaccine antibody response changes over time.”
 In addition to NIBIB, the research team includes scientists from the National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID), the National Center for Advancing Translational Sciences (NCATS); and the Frederick National Laboratory for Cancer Research, sponsored by the National Cancer Institute (NCI). Their report in the June 22, 2021, early online issue of  Science Translational Medicine represents the first data from the 12-month NIH study that was launched in April 2020.
 “A hallmark of the coronavirus pandemic is that there are people infected with the virus that causes COVID-19 who have few or no symptoms,” said Matthew J. Memoli, M.D., M.S., director, Clinical Studies Unit, Laboratory of Infectious Diseases, NIAID. “While counting the numbers of symptomatic people in the United States is essential to contend with the impact of the pandemic and public health response, gaining a full appreciation of the COVID-19 prevalence requires counting the people who are undiagnosed.”
 COVID-19 illness can range from short-lived cough, fatigue and fever to severe illness that could lead to hospitalization and death. Asymptomatic SARS-CoV-2 infection, which is rarely diagnosed, is a silent source of viral spread. While not causing overt signs of illness, undiagnosed infection poses a danger to the public and is a critical factor necessitating public health resources and strategies for addressing the pandemic.
 The team recruited over 240,000 volunteers from across the country, then selected 8,058 individuals from that pool through quota sampling to ensure that their study cohort was representative of the U.S. population. Each participant received a sample kit to return to NIH with a dab of dried blood or had blood drawn at NIH, most of which they supplied in an 11-week span between May 10 and July 31. Participants also completed a questionnaire that enabled further analysis of the study results.
 The researchers used an enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay to detect antibodies that recognize protein regions from the SARS-CoV-2 virus, referred to as antigens. Antibodies in a blood sample can bind to these antigens. The team found that 304 of the approximately 8,000 blood samples were seropositive, meaning that they contained antibodies against the SARS-CoV-2 virus. The researchers estimated that 4.6% of U.S. adults had undiagnosed COVID-19 during their study period. They determined that each diagnosed case of COVID-19 corresponds to an estimated 4.8 undiagnosed cases of the disease during this time frame.
  the youngest participants—those between the ages of 18 and 44—had the highest estimated seropositivity, at 5.9%,
    participants in the Mid-Atlantic and Northeast regions had the highest rates (8.6% and 7.5%, respectively), and participants in the Midwest had the lowest rates (1.6%),
    Black/African American respondents had the highest estimated seropositivity rate (14.2%), followed by Native American/Alaska Native (6.8%), Hispanic (6.1%), white/Caucasian (2.5%), and Asian (2%) respondents.
 “The estimate of COVID-19 cases in the United States in mid-July 2020, 3 million in a population of 330 million, should be revised upwards by almost 20 million when the percent of asymptomatic positive results is included,” said senior co-author Kaitlyn Sadtler, Ph.D., chief of the NIBIB Section on Immunoengineering. “This wide gap between the known cases at the time and these asymptomatic infections has implications not only for retrospectively understanding this pandemic, but future pandemic preparedness.”
 The researchers are currently following up with the enrolled participants to evaluate the six- and 12-month status of seroprevalence; it will include new analyses to differentiate antibodies from infection versus antibodies from vaccination, as well as antibody reactivity to variants of concern.
 The new report is part of the NIH SARS-CoV-2 Seroprevalence Project. The collaboration includes research groups led by Matthew J. Memoli, M.D., at NIAID; Matthew D. Hall, Ph.D., Early Translation Branch at NCATS; Dominic Esposito, Ph.D., Protein Expression Laboratory at the Frederick National Laboratory for Cancer Research; and Sadtler, Section on Immunoengineering, NIBIB.
 This research was supported in part by the NIH Intramural Research Program, funded in part under contract number HHSN261200800001E, 75N91019D00024, Task Order No. 75N91019F00130, Clinical and Translational Science Awards Program grants UL1TR003096 (Robert P. Kimberly, M.D., University of Alabama at Birmingham) and UL1TR001857 (Steven E. Reis, M.D., University of Pittsburgh).
 About the National Institute of Biomedical Imaging and Bioengineering (NIBIB): NIBIB’s mission is to improve health by leading the development and accelerating the application of biomedical technologies. The Institute is committed to integrating engineering and physical science with biology and medicine to advance our understanding of disease and its prevention, detection, diagnosis, and treatment. NIBIB supports emerging technology research and development within its internal laboratories and through grants, collaborations, and training. More information is available at the  NIBIB website.
 About the National Institutes of Health (NIH):NIH, the nation's medical research agency, includes 27 Institutes and Centers and is a component of the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services. NIH is the primary federal agency conducting and supporting basic, clinical, and translational medical research, and is investigating the causes, treatments, and cures for both common and rare diseases. For more information about NIH and its programs, visit  www.nih.gov.
title: NIH研究表明Covid-19患病率远远超过早期大流行病
title_en: NIH study suggests Covid-19 prevalence far exceeded early pandemic cases
